title: Untractable SPAM
creator: lanyi234
content: ThB & cPanel filters (a) don't catch all SPAM and (b) send legitimate mail to the Junk folder. Together, the 2 problems cause a large amount of work. Because of (b), I must regularly go through the Junk folder. Because of (a), the Junk folder contains a huge number of messages. (a) Messages from serial spammers trying to spoof Costco, Marriott, AAA, Blue Cross, etc. that I have flagged as SPAM dozens of times IN ALL THEIR VARIATIONS, still appear both in the Junk folder and in the Inbox (including messages with the red SPAM marker turned on, which to remove from the Inbox I must first click to mark them as non-S{AM, then click again to mark them as SPAM). (b) Messages from senders who have sent hundreds of messages before that have gotten through and who are in my contacts list regularly and in significant numbers end up in the Junk folder. I tried to set up filters in Spam Assassin. They made no difference. I tried to set up e-mail filters in cPanel, which entirely removed messages before they even reached ThB. These were quite effective, but although I created them very carefully using regex to capture every variation of COSTCO spelled with Os and 9s, etc., they also ended up removing legitimate messages (and none of my senders mentions AAA or BlueCross in their messages). I really have no clue how to proceed. None of the advice that AI or ISP support provided helped. Thank you for taking the time.

title: Thunderbird hangs my machine periodically
creator: john_fowler_son
content:
Thunderbird causes my machine to 'hang' from time to time, often for up to a second and usually a number of times in quick succession on each ocasion. Sometimes this happens frequently - say every 5 or 10 minutes - sometimes only after an hour or more since the past occurrance. My machine hangs completely - nothing will work, mouse, keyboard, sound, other apps etc. If I happen to be typing (into any app - including Thunderbird - when this happens, input ceases, the cursor freezes and - if the problem persists for a second or two, Windows reports "[app] is not responding" I cannot be certain, but I believe the problem occurs whilst messages are being retrieved.

This is a very serious problem as it means that I can never leave TB running as the problems it causes are not acceptable. This means I cannot deal with incoming messages without periodically re-starting TB - which is an utter PIA.

I am running Win 11 Professional on a 2-year old machine with 64 Gb of memory. I have run TB in Troubleshoot mode, I have confirmed that the problem occurs regardless or whatever other apps are running (or none) and I have proven conclusively that no other app causes any kind of similar problem.

Looking forward yto hearing if others have had the same issue and what can be done about it.

title: still cannot send out emails following a response from Mozilla support
creator: jamd.eshore
content:
Thank you for answering my request, Matt, but I cannot send my response via email. So I am copying and pasting what I tried to respond to Matt. Hopefully he can still access my original message if it is needed.

Hi Matt, Thank you for the prompt reply. Unfortunately F10 did nothing when I pressed on it with the cursor in the compose area. No menu bar came up. Nothing changed.

Yes, I guess I did disable the composition toolbar unintentionally as I did R click on the 2 choices (menu bar and composition toolbar) and it made them both go away and no options came up, with nothing left behind in that 'sent' area. (In response to you, it did go 'send later' in gray first and there had been some spotty internet access then but internet did resume fine, but the 'send later' stayed gray, but then disappeared as above.) The email addresses I was using were all current operational addresses.

So I need to get composition toolbar option back up, so I can respond to and send out emails. This is a major problem. I now have no way of responding to emails.

Thanks once again, Mary On 21/03/2026 3:35 a.m., Mozilla Support Forum wrote: > Mozilla Support > It no longer shows a 'sent' option at the top left corner when I am replying. I deleted it by mistake and I need it back > Matt > Mar 21, 2026, 4:35:00 AM > > If the send icon turned grey your computer was not connected to the internet, or you had placed Thunderbird in offline mode (but in that those cases it should change to send later not just go grey) SO just grey usually means that the email address entered in the to is simply not valid. Often there is a space before or after the entry or you have used an address book entry that does not actually have an email address. > > I guess you have somehow managed to disable the composition toolbar by right clicking on the toolbar or menu. If you have neither of those toolbar to right click on press the F10 key in the compose window to make a menu bar visible to right click on >
